user_request: Draft a suit for damages for defamation against a Defendant who published false and malicious allegations on Facebook damaging the Plaintiff's business reputation. Reproduce the defamatory statements in the body of the plaint. Plead malice, publication to third parties, loss of goodwill, and mental agony. Claim Rs.10,00,000/- as damages. Include prayer for permanent injunction restraining further publication on Facebook or any other social media platform. Draft suitable for filing before the District Court. Plead limitation correctly for defamation suits. Include cause of action paragraph with accrual date, continuing nature of cause of action, valuation, court fee, and proper verification clause.

facts.summary: The Plaintiff seeks damages for defamation against a Defendant who published false and malicious allegations on Facebook, damaging the Plaintiff's business reputation. The Plaintiff claims loss of goodwill and mental agony, seeking Rs. 10,00,000 in damages and a permanent injunction against further publication.

slots.value: Facebook or any other social media platform

Plaintiff and Defendant names and addresses
assumptions: Suit is filed within the limitation period from the date of publication
Jurisdiction lies with the specified District Court

(b)Facts not yet material to a case. The pleadings should only contain such facts as are material at the 
present stage of the suit. It is not proper to anticipate the answer of the adversary. To do so is like leaping 
before one comes to the stile. 34 Similarly, a defendant should not plead to any matter which is not alleged 
against him. Thus, if a defendant is sued for slander, the only way to meet the plaintiffs claim is to plead (1) 
that the words in question were not spoken and published, or (2) that they were spoken and published and 
are true, or (3) that they were spoken and published and are privileged. If, instead of so doing, he pleads 
that he did not say the words alleged in the plaint, but that he said something else; and that the something

draft_artifacts.title: PLAINT IN A SUIT FOR DAMAGES FOR DEFAMATION AND PERMANENT INJUNCTION
draft_artifacts.text:
IN THE COURT OF THE LEARNED DISTRICT JUDGE AT {{DISTRICT_PLACE}}, {{STATE}}

Civil Suit No. {{SUIT_NO}} of {{YEAR}}

IN THE MATTER OF:

{{PLAINTIFF_NAME}},
Age: {{PLAINTIFF_AGE}} years,
Occupation: {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}},
Address: {{PLAINTIFF_ADDRESS}},
…Plaintiff

VERSUS

{{DEFENDANT_NAME}},
Age: {{DEFENDANT_AGE}} years,
Occupation: {{DEFENDANT_OCCUPATION}},
Address: {{DEFENDANT_ADDRESS}},
…Defendant

PLAINT UNDER THE CIVIL PROCEDURE CODE, 1908 FOR DAMAGES FOR DEFAMATION AND FOR PERMANENT INJUNCTION

MOST RESPECTFULLY SHOWETH:

1. Parties
   a) The Plaintiff is an individual/concern engaged in the business of {{PLAINTIFF_BUSINESS_DESCRIPTION}} and has, prior to the acts complained of herein, enjoyed goodwill and reputation amongst customers, vendors, and members of the public.
   b) The Defendant is an individual who, acting with malice and without any lawful justification, published and caused to be published defamatory statements against the Plaintiff on the social media platform Facebook.

2. Jurisdiction
   This Hon’ble Court has territorial and pecuniary jurisdiction to entertain and try the present suit as the cause of action arose within the territorial jurisdiction of this Hon’ble Court, including but not limited to the publication and dissemination of the impugned statements and the consequent injury to the Plaintiff’s business reputation within {{DISTRICT_PLACE}}. The suit is valued within the pecuniary jurisdiction of this Hon’ble Court.

3. Facts Leading to the Suit
   a) The Plaintiff states that on or about {{PUBLICATION_DATE}} (hereinafter “the Date of Publication”), the Defendant published and/or caused to be published on Facebook false, malicious, and defamatory allegations concerning the Plaintiff.
   b) The Defendant’s said publication was made on Facebook in such a manner that it was accessible to, and was in fact read by, third parties including the Plaintiff’s customers, business associates, and members of the public.
   c) The Plaintiff states that the Defendant’s publication was not made in good faith, was not fair comment, and was not privileged; rather, it was motivated by malice and an intent to lower the Plaintiff’s reputation in the estimation of right-thinking members of society and to harm the Plaintiff’s business.

4. Defamatory Statements (Verbatim Reproduction)
   The Plaintiff reproduces herein the defamatory statements published by the Defendant on Facebook (as available from the impugned post(s)/comment(s)/caption(s) dated {{PUBLICATION_DATE}}), which are false and malicious. The Defendant stated inter alia as follows:

   i) “{{DEFAMATORY_STATEMENT_1}}”
   ii) “{{DEFAMATORY_STATEMENT_2}}”
   iii) “{{DEFAMATORY_STATEMENT_3}}”

   The above statements, taken in their plain and natural meaning and/or by innuendo, impute dishonest and/or disreputable conduct to the Plaintiff, are calculated to injure the Plaintiff’s reputation, and have in fact caused reputational harm.

5. Publication to Third Parties
   a) The Plaintiff states that the Defendant’s said statements were published on Facebook, a platform inherently designed for dissemination to multiple users.
   b) The Defendant’s publication was made available to third parties and was capable of being shared, reposted, commented upon, and otherwise further disseminated.
   c) The Plaintiff states that the publication was in fact viewed by third parties including {{THIRD_PARTIES_DESCRIPTION}}.

6. Falsity and Malice
   a) The impugned statements are false to the knowledge of the Defendant and were published with reckless disregard for the truth.
   b) The Defendant acted with malice, intending to damage the Plaintiff’s standing and business reputation, and to cause the Plaintiff mental agony.

7. Loss of Goodwill, Business Reputation, and Mental Agony
   a) As a direct and proximate consequence of the Defendant’s defamatory publication, the Plaintiff has suffered loss of goodwill and business reputation.
   b) The Plaintiff states that the Plaintiff’s customers and business associates have raised queries, expressed doubts, and/or reduced business dealings with the Plaintiff after the impugned publication.
   c) The Plaintiff has also suffered mental agony, stress, humiliation, and loss of peace of mind due to the Defendant’s malicious acts.
   d) The Plaintiff craves leave to rely upon matters in aggravation of damages and such further particulars as may be permitted and proved at trial.

8. Cause of Action
   a) The underlying wrongful act/obligation arose on the Date of Publication, i.e., {{PUBLICATION_DATE}}, when the Defendant published the false and malicious statements on Facebook concerning the Plaintiff.
   b) The right to sue accrued on {{ACCRUAL_DATE}} upon the said publication coming to the knowledge of third parties and causing injury to the Plaintiff’s reputation and business.
   c) The cause of action is of a continuing nature to the extent that the impugned content remains available on Facebook and/or continues to be accessible, viewed, shared, and republished, causing recurring injury. Without prejudice, the Plaintiff states that each continued display and/or further sharing constitutes fresh and recurring injury.

9. Limitation
   The Plaintiff states that the present suit is filed within the period of limitation applicable to civil suits for defamation, i.e., {{LIMITATION_PERIOD}} from the date the cause of action accrued (Article {{LIMITATION_ARTICLE}} of the Limitation Act, 1963). The suit is therefore within limitation. The Plaintiff craves leave to provide further particulars, if required.

10. Valuation and Court Fee
   a) For the purposes of jurisdiction and court fee, the Plaintiff values the suit for damages and injunction at Rs. 10,00,000/- (Rupees Ten Lakhs Only).
   b) The requisite court fee is paid/shall be paid in accordance with the applicable Court Fees law. As jurisdiction-specific rates are not presently available in the provided context, the court fee is stated as {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}}, to be verified and paid at the time of filing.

11. No Other Proceedings
   The Plaintiff states that no other suit or proceeding between the parties on the same cause of action and for the same relief is pending before any court, to the best of the Plaintiff’s knowledge.

12. Reliefs Sought (Prayer)
   In view of the facts and circumstances stated hereinabove, it is most respectfully prayed that this Hon’ble Court may be pleased to:

   a) Pass a decree in favour of the Plaintiff and against the Defendant for recovery of damages/compensation amounting to Rs. 10,00,000/- (Rupees Ten Lakhs Only) for defamation, loss of goodwill and business reputation, and mental agony;

   b) Pass a decree of permanent injunction restraining the Defendant, his agents, servants, associates, and/or any person acting through or under him from publishing, reposting, sharing, or causing to be published, reposted, shared, or otherwise disseminated any defamatory content against the Plaintiff on Facebook or any other social media platform;

   c) Direct the Defendant to delete/remove the impugned defamatory post(s)/content from Facebook and to desist from any further publication of the same or similar defamatory statements (as may be permissible within the scope of permanent injunction);

   d) Award costs of the suit in favour of the Plaintiff and against the Defendant; and

   e) Grant such other and further reliefs as this Hon’ble Court may deem fit and proper in the facts and circumstances of the case.

LIST OF DOCUMENTS / ENCLOSURES
(Labels for identification purposes)

Annexure P-1: Copy/screenshot/printout of the Defendant’s Facebook post(s)/publication dated {{PUBLICATION_DATE}} containing the defamatory statements.
Annexure P-2: Proof of URL / link details / page identification relating to the impugned Facebook content (if available).
Annexure P-3: Documents evidencing Plaintiff’s business reputation/goodwill (registration, invoices, promotional material, customer communications) (if available).
Annexure P-4: Documents evidencing loss of business/goodwill (communications, cancellations, reduced orders) (if available).
Annexure P-5: Any legal notice/demand issued to the Defendant and reply, if any (if issued).

VERIFICATION

I, {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, the Plaintiff above named, do hereby verify that the contents of paragraphs 1 to {{PARA_FACTS_END}} are true and correct to my knowledge and belief, and the contents of paragraphs {{PARA_LEGAL_START}} to {{PARA_LEGAL_END}} are based on legal advice which I believe to be true. Nothing material has been concealed therefrom.

Verified at {{DISTRICT_PLACE}} on this ___ day of {{MONTH}} {{YEAR}}.

__________________________
{{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}
Plaintiff

Through Counsel:
{{COUNSEL_NAME}}
{{COUNSEL_ENROLMENT_NO}}
{{COUNSEL_ADDRESS}}
{{COUNSEL_MOBILE_EMAIL}}

   This Hon’ble Court has territorial and pecuniary jurisdiction to entertain and try the present suit as the cause of action arose within the territorial jurisdiction of this Hon’ble Court, including but not limited to the publication and dissemination of the impugned statements and the consequent injury to the Plaintiff’s business reputation within {{DISTRICT_PLACE}}. The suit is valued within the pecuniary jurisdiction of this Hon’ble Court.

3. Facts Leading to the Suit
   a) The Plaintiff states that on or about {{PUBLICATION_DATE}} (hereinafter “the Date of Publication”), the Defendant published and/or caused to be published on Facebook false, malicious, and defamatory allegations concerning the Plaintiff.
   b) The said publication was made on Facebook in such a manner that it was accessible to, and was in fact read by, third parties including the Plaintiff’s customers, business associates, and members of the public.
   c) The Plaintiff states that the Defendant’s publication was not made in good faith, was not fair comment, and was not privileged; rather, it was motivated by malice and an intent to lower the Plaintiff’s reputation in the estimation of right-thinking members of society and to harm the Plaintiff’s business.

4. Defamatory Statements (Verbatim Reproduction)
   The Plaintiff reproduces herein the defamatory statements published by the Defendant on Facebook (as available from the impugned post(s)/comment(s)/caption(s) dated {{PUBLICATION_DATE}}), which are false and malicious. The Defendant stated inter alia as follows:

   i) “{{DEFAMATORY_STATEMENT_1}}”
   ii) “{{DEFAMATORY_STATEMENT_2}}”
   iii) “{{DEFAMATORY_STATEMENT_3}}”

   The above statements, taken in their plain and natural meaning and/or by innuendo, impute dishonest and/or disreputable conduct to the Plaintiff, are calculated to injure the Plaintiff’s reputation, and have in fact caused reputational harm.

5. Publication to Third Parties
   a) The Plaintiff states that the Defendant’s said statements were published on Facebook, a platform inherently designed for dissemination to multiple users.
   b) The Defendant’s publication was made available to third parties and was capable of being shared, reposted, commented upon, and otherwise further disseminated.
   c) The Plaintiff states that the publication was in fact viewed by third parties including {{THIRD_PARTIES_DESCRIPTION}}.

6. Falsity and Malice
   a) The impugned statements are false to the knowledge of the Defendant and were published with reckless disregard for the truth.
   b) The Defendant acted with malice, intending to damage the Plaintiff’s standing and business reputation, and to cause the Plaintiff mental agony.

7. Loss of Goodwill, Business Reputation, and Mental Agony
   a) As a direct and proximate consequence of the Defendant’s defamatory publication, the Plaintiff has suffered loss of goodwill and business reputation.
   b) The Plaintiff states that the Plaintiff’s customers and business associates have raised queries, expressed doubts, and/or reduced business dealings with the Plaintiff after the impugned publication.
   c) The Plaintiff has also suffered mental agony, stress, humiliation, and loss of peace of mind due to the Defendant’s malicious acts.
   d) The Plaintiff craves leave to rely upon matters in aggravation of damages and such further particulars as may be permitted and proved at trial.

8. Cause of Action
   a) The underlying wrongful act occurred on the Date of Publication, i.e., {{PUBLICATION_DATE}}, when the Defendant published the false and malicious statements on Facebook concerning the Plaintiff.
   b) Accordingly, the right to sue accrued on {{PUBLICATION_DATE}} (hereinafter, “Accrual Date”).
   c) The cause of action is pleaded as continuing/recurring to the extent the impugned content remains available on Facebook and/or continues to be accessible, viewed, shared, and republished, causing recurring injury. Without prejudice, the Plaintiff states that each continued display and/or further sharing constitutes fresh and recurring injury.

9. Limitation
   The Plaintiff states that the present suit is filed within the period of limitation applicable to civil suits for defamation under the Limitation Act, 1963. The Plaintiff states that the limitation is to be computed from the date when the right to sue accrued, i.e., from the Accrual Date stated in Paragraph 8 above. The Plaintiff pleads that the suit is within limitation.

   The Plaintiff states that the exact limitation period/provision relied upon is {{LIMITATION_PROVISION}} providing {{LIMITATION_PERIOD}}, which is to be verified prior to filing, and the Plaintiff craves leave to amend/clarify the said particulars if required.

10. Valuation and Court Fee
   a) For the purposes of jurisdiction and court fee, the Plaintiff values the suit for damages and injunction at Rs. 10,00,000/- (Rupees Ten Lakhs Only).
   b) The requisite court fee is to be paid in accordance with the applicable Court Fees law/schedule for the concerned State. As jurisdiction-specific rates are not presently available in the provided context, the court fee is stated as {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}} and the basis as {{COURT_FEE_BASIS}}, both to be verified and paid at the time of filing.

11. No Other Proceedings
   The Plaintiff states that no other suit or proceeding between the parties on the same cause of action and for the same relief is pending before any court, to the best of the Plaintiff’s knowledge.

12. Reliefs Sought (Prayer)
   In view of the facts and circumstances stated hereinabove, it is most respectfully prayed that this Hon’ble Court may be pleased to:

   a) Pass a decree in favour of the Plaintiff and against the Defendant for recovery of damages/compensation amounting to Rs. 10,00,000/- (Rupees Ten Lakhs Only) for defamation, loss of goodwill and business reputation, and mental agony;

   b) Pass a decree of permanent injunction restraining the Defendant, his agents, servants, associates, and/or any person acting through or under him from publishing, reposting, sharing, or causing to be published, reposted, shared, or otherwise disseminated any defamatory content against the Plaintiff on Facebook or any other social media platform;

   c) Direct the Defendant to delete/remove the impugned defamatory post(s)/content from Facebook and to desist from any further publication of the same or similar defamatory statements (as may be permissible within the scope of permanent injunction);

   d) Award costs of the suit in favour of the Plaintiff and against the Defendant; and

   e) Grant such other and further reliefs as this Hon’ble Court may deem fit and proper in the facts and circumstances of the case.

(Labels for identification purposes)

Annexure P-1: Copy/screenshot/printout of the Defendant’s Facebook post(s)/publication dated {{PUBLICATION_DATE}} containing the defamatory statements.
Annexure P-2: Proof of URL / link details / page identification relating to the impugned Facebook content (if available).
Annexure P-3: Documents evidencing Plaintiff’s business reputation/goodwill (registration, invoices, promotional material, customer communications) (if available).
Annexure P-4: Documents evidencing loss of business/goodwill (communications, cancellations, reduced orders) (if available).
Annexure P-5: Any legal notice/demand issued to the Defendant and reply, if any (if issued).

placeholders_used.reason: Accrual date must be tied to publication date for defamation; actual publication date not provided in intake.
Exact Limitation Act provision/article for civil defamation is not available in RAG_CHUNKS; must be verified from authoritative text before filing.
Exact limitation period for civil defamation is not available in RAG_CHUNKS; must be verified from authoritative text before filing.
COURT_FEE_CONTEXT contains no rates; exact court fee must be computed per State Court Fees schedule before filing.
Court fee computation basis (ad valorem/fixed + schedule entry) not available in COURT_FEE_CONTEXT; must be verified before filing.

blocking_issues.issue: Cause of action accrual date is not correctly anchored/pleaded: accrual is left as a placeholder and is framed as dependent on ‘coming to the knowledge of third parties’, rather than clearly anchoring the right to sue to the wrongful publication date (and, if relied upon, plaintiff’s knowledge date) with specific dates from facts.
blocking_issues.fix: Plead a specific accrual date tied to the defamatory publication event (date of posting) as the primary accrual; if plaintiff first became aware later, plead that date as an alternative/without prejudice with particulars. Replace {{ACCRUAL_DATE}} with a single coherent accrual date tied to facts, or use a clearly flagged placeholder {{ACCRUAL_DATE=PUBLICATION_DATE}} pending confirmation. Do not make accrual contingent on third-party knowledge.

blocking_issues.issue: Limitation paragraph cites ‘Article {{LIMITATION_ARTICLE}} of the Limitation Act, 1963’ and a generic ‘{{LIMITATION_PERIOD}}’ without verified statutory support from RAG_CHUNKS; this is a statute/article reference without RAG support.
blocking_issues.fix: Remove the unverified Article reference and replace with placeholders for the exact limitation provision/article and period to be verified (e.g., ‘under the applicable provision of the Limitation Act, 1963: {{LIMITATION_PROVISION}} providing {{LIMITATION_PERIOD}} from {{ACCRUAL_DATE}}’). Ensure limitation is pleaded from the accrual date (publication) and include an alternative pleading for continuing/recurring publication if relied upon.

blocking_issues.issue: Court fee is left as an uncomputed placeholder and the draft does not compute fee based on jurisdictional schedule; for a quantified civil damages suit, filing with an unspecified fee can lead to office objections.
blocking_issues.fix: Use COURT_FEE_CONTEXT to compute ad valorem/fixed court fee for Rs.10,00,000/- and state the computed amount with basis; if COURT_FEE_CONTEXT is not actually provided with rates, keep {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}} but also add {{COURT_FEE_BASIS}} and mark as to-be-verified before filing, and avoid stating/representing ‘requisite court fee is paid’ unless it is paid.

non_blocking_issues.issue: Annexure/document labels are not referenced in the body text; only a generic reference to ‘impugned post(s)’ is used. This can create document-reference inconsistency at scrutiny stage though labels exist in list.
non_blocking_issues.fix: Add explicit references in the facts/defamatory statements paragraphs to ‘Annexure P-1’, ‘Annexure P-2’, etc., or remove the labels and use a consistent scheme throughout.

non_blocking_issues.issue: Verification clause uses unresolved paragraph-range placeholders; courts often require precise paragraph numbers or a simpler verification covering the whole plaint.
non_blocking_issues.fix: Replace {{PARA_FACTS_END}}, {{PARA_LEGAL_START}}, {{PARA_LEGAL_END}} with fixed paragraph ranges once final numbering is settled, or use a standard verification stating ‘contents of paragraphs 1 to 12 are true…’ as applicable.

non_blocking_issues.issue: Title line ‘PLAINT UNDER THE CIVIL PROCEDURE CODE, 1908’ is generic; CPC is not typically cited as the substantive basis for defamation and may be unnecessary in the caption.
non_blocking_issues.fix: Consider revising caption to ‘Plaint for damages for defamation and permanent injunction’ without asserting a CPC basis beyond procedure.

unsupported_statements.statement: “The present suit is filed within the period of limitation applicable to civil suits for defamation, i.e., {{LIMITATION_PERIOD}} from the date the cause of action accrued (Article {{LIMITATION_ARTICLE}} of the Limitation Act, 1963).”
unsupported_statements.reason: Specific limitation period and the Limitation Act article number are not supported by RAG_CHUNKS; LEGAL_RESEARCH_CONTEXT contains non-authoritative web snippets and cannot be treated as verified statutory text.

unsupported_statements.statement: “The cause of action is of a continuing nature to the extent that the impugned content remains available on Facebook… each continued display and/or further sharing constitutes fresh and recurring injury.”
unsupported_statements.reason: No facts are pleaded showing the post remained available, dates of continued availability, or instances of re-sharing/republishing; ‘continuing cause’ assertion needs factual particulars to avoid being conclusory.
